items.english: Green apple, but green apples / apple fruit.
Red tomato, but red tomatoes.
Big onion, but big onions.
Green cucumber, but green cucumbers.
Good loaf of bread, but good bread.
Good cheese, but good cheese in collective use.
Fresh fish, but fish in collective use.
Hardworking bee, but hardworking bees.
Small mosquito, but small mosquitoes.
Dirty fly, but dirty flies.
I need apples.
We need oranges.
I am going to buy a kilo of mandarins.
I would like half a kilo of oranges.

items.maltese: Għandi bżonn kilo u nofs lanġas.
items.english: I need one and a half kilos of pears.
I am going to buy some aubergines.
I want some garlic.
We have a lot of oranges at home.
Today I bought a lot of watermelons.

items.maltese: Għandi żewġ basliet u erba' tadamiet.
items.english: I have two onions and four tomatoes.

items.maltese: Xtrajt żewġ larinġiet u żewġ tewmiet.
items.english: I bought two oranges and two garlic bulbs.

items.maltese: Fil-lista tal-ħaxix ktibt tadam, ħass, brunġiel u ħjar.
items.english: In the vegetable list I wrote tomatoes, lettuce, aubergines, and cucumbers.

items.maltese: Fil-lista tal-frott ktibt tuffieħ, larinġ, mandolin u lanġas.
items.english: In the fruit list I wrote apples, oranges, mandarins, and pears.
I like to buy fresh fruit.
My mother likes to buy fresh vegetables.

items.maltese: Fil-kċina għandna lumi, tuffieħ u bajd.
items.english: In the kitchen we have lemons, apples, and eggs.

items.maltese: Fil-friġġ hemm ġobon tajjeb u ħut frisk.
items.english: In the fridge there is good cheese and fresh fish.
At the market I saw bees and flies.

items.maltese: Ma rridx larinġ għax ma jidhirx frisk.
items.english: I do not want oranges because they do not look fresh.

items.maltese: Illum mort is-suq u xtrajt ftit frott u ħaxix.
items.english: Today I went to the market and bought some fruit and vegetables.
